fact: September 23 is almost always the exact moment when day and night become perfectly equal all over Earth - it's called the autumn equinox in the north and spring equinox in the south!
On this date, the sun rises due east and sets due west no matter where you live on the planet - your compass and the sun are best friends today!
Squirrels go absolutely bonkers around September 23, frantically burying up to 25 nuts per day because their tiny brains know winter is coming!
In the Northern Hemisphere, leaves start changing colors right around now because trees are basically going to sleep and stop making the green stuff called chlorophyll.
September 23 marks the beginning of Libra season - and Libras are obsessed with balance, which is perfect since day and night are balanced too!
Monarch butterflies are in the middle of their epic 3,000-mile journey south right now, and some of the ones flying today will live 8 times longer than their great-grandparents!
This date falls exactly 266 days into the year - which happens to be the same number of days a human baby grows inside its mom!
In Australia and other southern places, September 23 means spring is starting and flowers are popping up everywhere after their long winter nap!
The moon looks extra bright around this time because autumn air is clearer and less humid than summer air - it's like nature cleaned its windows!
Bears in cold places are eating like absolute maniacs right now, gobbling up 20,000 calories per day to get ready for hibernation - that's like eating 40 hamburgers!
September 23 is exactly 99 days before New Year's Eve, making it the perfect time to start planning something awesome for next year!
Spiders seem to appear everywhere around this date because they're actually just getting bigger and easier to spot - they've been growing all summer long!
